scenarios.scenario-1: Verified user assignments persist across service restart and can be looked up by email.
scenarios.scenario-2: Bad DETS path or open failure is surfaced before issuance returns success.
scenarios.scenario-3: Concurrent writes do not corrupt state or lose assignments.

critical-invariants.invariant-1: One email maps deterministically to one stored assignment.
critical-invariants.invariant-2: Persistence happens before final user success is rendered.

side-effects.effect-1: Opens and writes DETS files on persistent storage.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Mask emails and never log full proxy URLs from registry operations.

verification-levels.wave-level: Restart persistence and corruption handling tests.
verification-levels.phase-level: Issued proxy remains stable across deploy restarts.
